data.title: Bitcoin's Historical Interaction with Production Cost
data.description: Crypto KOL Satoshi Flipper posted on X about Bitcoin's historical price movements in relation to its production cost. According to the post, Bitcoin has touched its production cost average nine times over the past 14 years. On four occasions, the price dipped below this average but did not remain there for long.

data.title: USMCA Pact's Importance to U.S. and Mexican Economies
data.description: The economic integration between the United States and Mexico has reached a level where dismantling the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) would have significant repercussions for Washington. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the potential impact of unraveling the trade agreement is underestimated by U.S. President Donald Trump. The USMCA, which replaced the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), plays a crucial role in maintaining economic stability and fostering trade relations between the two nations. The agreement's dissolution could lead to economic disruptions, affecting various sectors reliant on cross-border trade. Experts emphasize the importance of the USMCA in sustaining the economic ties that have developed over the years, warning against underestimating the potential fallout of its termination.

data.title: Bitcoin's Historical Pattern Suggests Potential Bullish Phase Ahead
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X, highlighting a historical pattern in Bitcoin's performance. The cryptocurrency has experienced five consecutive months of decline, a situation reminiscent of a previous period that led to a significant bullish phase. Observers are speculating whether this pattern might repeat, potentially signaling an upcoming surge in Bitcoin's value. The discussion centers around the possibility of a rebound, drawing parallels to past market behavior. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation, considering various factors that could influence Bitcoin's trajectory in the coming months.

data.title: Verizon Plans Sale of Euro and Sterling Hybrid Bonds
data.description: Verizon Communications Inc. is preparing to issue euro and sterling denominated hybrid bonds on Wednesday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the telecommunications giant's strategic move to tap into the European debt market. This decision comes as part of Verizon's broader financial strategy to diversify its funding sources and optimize its capital structure. The hybrid bonds, which combine elements of both debt and equity, are expected to attract significant interest from investors seeking stable returns amid fluctuating market conditions. Verizon's initiative reflects a growing trend among U.S. companies to explore international bond markets, leveraging favorable interest rates and investor appetite for diversified financial instruments. The issuance is anticipated to bolster Verizon's financial flexibility and support its ongoing operational and strategic objectives.

data.title: Hong Kong-based Laure made a single purchase of $436 million in IBIT, while the US spot Bitcoin ETF saw an outflow of $104 million on Tuesday.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, the US spot Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of $104 million, reducing its total trading volume to approximately $3 billion. The SEC's Q4 holdings report showed that Hong Kong-based Laurore made a single purchase of $436 million worth of iShares Bitcoin ETF (IBIT); Jane Street bought $276 million worth of IBIT in Q4, becoming its second-largest buyer. Additionally, Mubadala Investment increased its IBIT holdings to 12.7 million shares, worth $630 million. During the same period, Brevan Howard reduced its IBIT holdings by approximately 85%, down to $273 million; Goldman Sachs reduced its IBIT holdings by approximately 40%, leaving a remaining holding of approximately $1 billion.

data.title: Zimbabwe's Tobacco Production Forecast to Exceed 360 Million Kilograms This Year
data.description: Zimbabwe anticipates a significant increase in tobacco production, projecting over 360 million kilograms for this year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's optimistic outlook for its tobacco industry. This forecast marks a substantial rise from previous years, reflecting improvements in agricultural practices and favorable weather conditions. Tobacco remains a crucial export commodity for Zimbabwe, contributing significantly to the nation's economy. The government and industry stakeholders are hopeful that this year's production will bolster economic growth and enhance the livelihoods of farmers across the country.

data.title: Bitcoin DeFi expands as Nimbus, Chimera set $15M partnership
data.description:
Nimbus Capital and Chimera Wallet, $15 million strategic partnership, Bitcoin DeFi infrastructure funds self-custody tooling; regulation cited, no validation.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-defi-expands-as-nimbus-chimera-set-15m-partnership/

data.title: Supreme Court Likely to Rule Against Trump Tariffs
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X that there is a 74% probability the Supreme Court will declare U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs illegal. This development could have significant implications for international trade and economic policy. The tariffs, which have been a contentious issue, are under scrutiny for their legality and impact on global markets. Observers are closely monitoring the situation as the Supreme Court's decision could reshape trade relations and economic strategies.

data.title: Criticism of McGlone's Podcast Appearances
data.description: Crypto KOL Satoshi Flipper posted on X, expressing criticism towards McGlone's frequent podcast appearances. The comment suggests that McGlone should improve his communication skills to better articulate his position and avoid sounding absurd during discussions. This remark highlights the importance of clear communication in media appearances, especially in the rapidly evolving crypto industry.

data.title: Germany Considers Social Media Ban for Minors Amid U.S. Concerns
data.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has expressed support for proposals to implement a social media ban for minors in Germany. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move could potentially create friction with the United States. The proposal aims to address concerns about the impact of social media on young users, but it may lead to diplomatic challenges between the two nations. The initiative reflects growing global scrutiny over the influence of social media platforms on youth.

data.title: Intesa Sanpaolo holds $96 million in Bitcoin ETFs.
data.description: According to its 13-F filing with the SEC, Intesa Sanpaolo SpA held $96 million in Bitcoin spot ETFs as of the end of last year, including $72.6 million in the ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF and $23.4 million in the iShares Bitcoin Trust. Intesa Sanpaolo SpA established its proprietary cryptocurrency trading desk within its corporate and investment banking arm in 2023 and completed its first €1 million Bitcoin spot purchase last year. In addition, the bank also holds $4.3 million in the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF and $184.6 million worth of Strategy Inc. put options. Its holdings also show stakes in Circle Internet Group Inc., Robinhood Markets Inc., BitMine Immersion Technologies Inc., Coinbase Global Inc., and ETHZilla Corp.

data.title: Kenya Plans Eurobond Buyback for 2032 and 2028 Maturities
data.description: Kenya has announced its intention to repurchase eurobonds set to mature in 2032 and 2028. Bloomberg posted on X, detailing the country's strategy to manage its debt obligations effectively. This move is part of Kenya's broader financial strategy to optimize its debt portfolio and reduce future liabilities. The buyback is expected to help stabilize the nation's economic outlook by addressing upcoming debt maturities. Kenya's government aims to improve fiscal sustainability and enhance investor confidence through this initiative. The decision reflects a proactive approach to managing external debt and ensuring long-term economic stability.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Zelensky Accuses Trump of Inappropriate Pressure in Ukraine-Russia Talks
data.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has accused U.S. President Donald Trump of exerting undue pressure during ongoing trilateral negotiations between the United States, Ukraine, and Russia in Geneva, Switzerland. According to Jin10, Zelensky stated that Ukraine will not accept any peace agreement that demands a unilateral withdrawal from the Donbas region and its transfer to Russian control. The discussions aim to address the ongoing conflict between Ukraine and Russia, but Zelensky's remarks highlight the complexities and challenges in reaching a resolution.

data.title: Canada's Housing Market Faces Challenges at Start of Year
data.description:
The Canadian housing market began the year with significant challenges, as reported by Bloomberg posted on X. The market experienced a slowdown due to various factors, including rising interest rates and economic uncertainties. Analysts noted that these conditions have led to decreased buyer activity and a cooling of home prices across the country.

Industry experts have observed that the market's sluggish start is a continuation of trends seen in the previous year, where affordability issues and tighter lending standards impacted potential homebuyers. Despite these hurdles, some regions have shown resilience, with certain areas experiencing stable demand and price levels.

The Canadian Real Estate Association has highlighted that while the market is facing headwinds, there are opportunities for recovery as economic conditions stabilize. The association suggests that potential buyers may find favorable conditions in the coming months, as sellers adjust to the new market dynamics.

Overall, the Canadian housing market's performance at the beginning of the year reflects broader economic challenges, but there remains optimism for improvement as the year progresses.

data.title: India Finalizes Trade Deals with EU and US, Boosting Global Trade Position
data.description:
India has successfully concluded significant trade agreements with the European Union and the United States in early 2026. According to NS3.AI, these agreements are set to substantially lower tariffs and expand market access, marking a crucial development in India's global trade strategy. The deals are expected to enhance export opportunities and increase trade volumes with these major economic entities.

Despite the positive outlook, the agreement with the United States has faced criticism due to its asymmetric terms and substantial procurement commitments. This has raised concerns about India's leadership role within the BRICS group, as the terms may complicate its position. Nonetheless, these trade deals represent a strategic shift for India, aiming to strengthen its economic ties with key global partners.

data.title: STOCKS | Apple’s Correlation with Nasdaq 100 Hits Lowest Level Since 2006
data.description: Apple's 40-day correlation with the Nasdaq 100 Index fell to 0.21 last week, marking its lowest point since 2006. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant shift in Apple's stock behavior. The decline in correlation suggests that Apple's stock movements are increasingly independent of the broader tech-heavy index, which could have implications for investors and market analysts. This change comes amid various market dynamics and economic factors influencing tech stocks. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation to understand the potential impacts on investment strategies and market predictions.

data.title: Bitwis has filed a prospectus with the SEC for a prediction market ETF linked to the 2028 U.S. presidential election.
data.description: Bitwise has filed a prospectus with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission outlining its plans for a prediction market ETF linked to the outcome of the 2028 U.S. presidential election. (TheBlock)

data.title: Nimbus Capital and Chimera Wallet Forge $15 Million Partnership to Enhance Bitcoin DeFi
data.description:
On February 18, Nimbus Capital announced a $15 million strategic partnership with Chimera Wallet, a non-custodial wallet built on Bitcoin's VTXO technology. According to BlockBeats, the collaboration aims to expand decentralized finance (DeFi) capabilities within the Bitcoin ecosystem and advance programmable financial tools on the Bitcoin network.

The partnership will integrate Bitcoin's underlying security with the programmable capabilities of the Arkade layer built on Bitcoin. This integration will offer users DeFi-related services such as asset exchange, lending mechanisms, liquidity functions, fiat on-ramps, and payment integration, all while maintaining self-custody of assets.

Claudio Levrini, founder and director of Chimera, stated that the collaboration combines financial expertise with Bitcoin infrastructure development, aiming to enhance Bitcoin's practical applications while ensuring self-custody. Robert Baker, managing partner at Nimbus Capital, noted that this move will facilitate further integration of Bitcoin in both institutional and decentralized settings.

Chimera plans to leverage this partnership to accelerate product development. This includes the global rollout of the Chimera Visa card to support BTC and fiat conversions, integration of digital gift cards and merchant gateway systems, enhancement of liquidity and cross-chain interoperability, and development of user-end features such as swaps, yield products, and portfolio management.

data.title: Binance Alpha's second wave of VELVET airdrop rewards is now available for collection.
data.description: Binance Alpha's second wave of Velvet (VELVET) airdrop rewards is now available for collection. Users with at least 242 Binance Alpha Points can claim 400 VELVET on a first-come, first-served basis. If the reward pool is not fully distributed, the points threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes. Claiming the airdrop requires 15 Binance Alpha Points, and users must confirm their claim on the Alpha event page within 24 hours; failure to do so will be considered a forfeiture.
